Metadata.ExomizerCommand: exomizer sfx 0x2304 "ENLIGHTENMENTDRUIDII-C000-2304" -Di_table_addr=0x02 -Di_irq_exit=0 -s "inc $d030 lsr $d011" -f "w1: bit $d011 bpl w1 w2: bit $d011 bmi w2 jsr $fda3 rol $d011 lda #$36 sta $01 jsr $c000 dec $d030" -x "inc $d020" -o "Enlightenment - Druid II (J1).prg"
Metadata.Notes:
Added an init routine at $C000 which: 1. Turns off interrupts from the two CIA chips and acks any pending CIA interrupts 2. Sets up the correct screen RAM and bitmap settings 3. Sets $DD01 (CIA#2 Port B) to $24 and $DD08, $DD09 and $DD0B (time of day values) to $01 (these were set during the original loading routine and allows the game to run properly) 4. Copies 512 bytes of data from $C06F to $0120 where it was after the original game had loaded 5. Sets $99 (current input device number) to the default of $00 (keyboard) and $9A (current output device number) to the default of $03 (screen) to ensure the game gets past the intro animation 6. Sets screen and border colours to black and clears colour RAM to allow the intro animation to display correctly (i.e. without garbage behind it) 7. It sets the stack pointer to $22 (i.e. below the data copied in step 4) 8. And finally jumps to the start of the game at $2304 (it doesn't use RTS as the stack was overwritten in step 4)
Note: the space used at $C000 in the original dump contained a BASIC program which seems to have been left over from the development process, so it was safe to overwrite.
